stimulus: Although former competitive athletes make up a small share of the total workforce in Canada, they hold a noticeably higher share of management positions in physical-therapy clinics. One possible explanation for this disproportionate representation is that competitive athletic careers familiarize people with structured rehabilitation protocols that are also central to physical-therapy clinic operations, and this familiarity thus ______blank
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: increases the number of physical-therapy management positions that require former athletic experience to perform.
encourages nonathletes applying for clinic management to pursue competitive sport instead.
alters the typical relationship between athletic careers and subsequent professional preferences.
makes physical-therapy management positions especially appealing to former competitive athletes.

rationale: Choice D is best. The text presents a population pattern (former athletes overrepresented in clinic management) and a proposed mechanism (familiarity with rehabilitation protocols). The most logical generalization is that this familiarity makes these management roles especially appealing to former athletes. Choice A flips cause and effect, making the jobs require athletic experience. Choice B is unsupported—nonathletes are not addressed. Choice C posits an "atypical" relationship the text does not establish.
